ddls:
CREATE TABLE "shows" (
  "show_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each show */,
  "show_name" TEXT /* Name of the show */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network where the show is broadcast */,
  "pe" REAL /* Program Execution (PE) rating of the show */,
  "grp" REAL /* Gross Rating Points (GRP) of the show */,
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the genre of the show */,
  "duration" INTEGER /* Duration of the show in minutes */,
  "air_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the show airs */,
  "rating" REAL /* Average rating of the show */,
  "total_views" INTEGER /* Total number of views */,
  "air_slot" TEXT /* Time slot during which the show airs (e.g., morning, evening) */,
  "season" INTEGER /* Season number of the show */,
  "episode" INTEGER /* Episode number within the season */,
  "show_type" TEXT /* Type of show (e.g., series, movie, special) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the show (e.g., ongoing, completed, canceled) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the show */,
  "poster_url" TEXT /* URL to the show's poster image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("show_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shows_genre_id FOREIGN KEY ("genre_id") REFERENCES genres ("genre_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_shows_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "networks" (
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each network */,
  "network_name" TEXT /* Name of the network */,
  "contact_person" TEXT /* Name of the primary contact person for the network */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Email address of the primary contact person */,
  "contact_phone" TEXT /* Phone number of the primary contact person */,
  "office_address" TEXT /* Physical address of the network's office */,
  "website" TEXT /* URL to the network's official website */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the network */,
  "logo_url" TEXT /* URL to the network's logo image */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "genres" (
  "genre_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each genre */,
  "genre_name" TEXT /* Name of the genre */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the genre */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("genre_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., administrator, analyst, network executive) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed) */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Date and time when the user account was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "show_ratings" (
  "rating_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each rating */,
  "show_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the show */,
  "rating_value" REAL /* The rating value */,
  "rating_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the rating was given */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the rating (e.g., critic, audience) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("rating_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_show_ratings_show_id FOREIGN KEY ("show_id") REFERENCES shows ("show_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "show_schedule" (
  "schedule_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each schedule entry */,
  "show_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the show */,
  "network_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the network */,
  "air_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the show airs */,
  "air_slot" TEXT /* Time slot during which the show airs (e.g., morning, evening) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("schedule_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_show_schedule_show_id FOREIGN KEY ("show_id") REFERENCES shows ("show_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_show_schedule_network_id FOREIGN KEY ("network_id") REFERENCES networks ("network_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_role_permissions" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id", "role_id", "permission_id")
)
